sourceMovie.title: Hackers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hackers for the same reason it broke through in 1995: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Iain Softley keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: We Die Young
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hackers clicked for you, We Die Young (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hackers?
faq.answer: Try We Die Young, American Psycho, Panic Room—each shares DNA with Hackers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hackers?
faq.answer: Hackers (1995) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hackers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
